equivalent to one year. Others progress the Sun and the M.C.
by one degree or 59'8" per year after birth. Still others take the
solar return or the lunar return or both. When we look at the
arcs of progression given by various authorities, we are bound
to be confused.
Before we proceed let us have a look at the confusion into
which the astrological authorities are pleased to throw us.
Frankland wants to apply 4 deg.n or 34 2!7 minutes per every
year after birth and also to apply 3 deg.f.! or 25 5n minutes
per year. Carter leaves it to us to choose one of these; and then
we have to combine it with one degree and 2 1/2 degrees
progression per year. He accepts Frankland's view that 4 deg./
7 arc is malefic while 3 deg.n measure is benefic. And in his
'Principles ofHoroscopic Delineation' he observes: "lfi aban
doned all others, I would at least retain the 1 deg. to a year
measure". Dutt proposed that 3 deg . 36' to be added per every
